benefits.p: Broccoli sprouts are exceptionally rich in glucoraphanin, which is converted into sulforaphane when the sprouts are chopped or chewed. Sulforaphane helps protect cells from oxidative stress by supporting the body's natural defence systems.

ref.s: Frontiers in Nutrition

benefits.p: Broccoli sprouts naturally provide vitamin C, which contributes to the normal function of the immune system while also supporting normal collagen formation and helping protect cells from oxidative stress.

ref.s: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements – Vitamin C Fact Sheet

benefits.p: Broccoli sprouts provide folate, an essential B vitamin that contributes to normal blood formation, healthy cell division and normal tissue growth during pregnancy.

ref.s: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements – Folate Fact Sheet

benefits.p: Hemp seeds are naturally rich in unsaturated fats, including omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids. These essential fats help maintain normal heart function as part of a balanced diet and contribute to overall cardiovascular health.

ref.s: NHS — Facts about fat

benefits.p: Hemp seeds provide high-quality plant protein containing all essential amino acids. Protein contributes to the maintenance of muscle mass and normal bones, making hemp seeds a nutritious addition to plant-based diets.

ref.s: European Food Information Council (EUFIC) — Plant-based protein: all you need to know to get enough of it

benefits.p: Hemp seeds are an excellent source of magnesium and phosphorus. Magnesium supports normal muscle and nervous system function, while both minerals contribute to normal energy metabolism and the maintenance of healthy bones.

ref.s: European Food Safety Authority (EFSA) — Dietary reference values for magnesium and phosphorus

benefits.p: Cinnamon naturally contains polyphenols that help protect cells from oxidative stress. Used as part of a varied diet, these plant compounds contribute to the spice's antioxidant capacity.

ref.s: National Center for Biotechnology Information – Polyphenols: Sources and Bioavailability

benefits.p: Cinnamon is a concentrated natural source of manganese, a trace mineral that contributes to normal energy metabolism, the maintenance of normal connective tissue and the protection of cells from oxidative stress.

ref.s: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements – Manganese Fact Sheet for Health Professionals

benefits.p: Cinnamon has been widely studied for its effects on glucose metabolism. As part of a balanced diet, it may help support normal blood glucose regulation, although it should not replace healthy lifestyle measures or medical treatment.

ref.s: Nutrients (2022) – Cinnamon as a Complementary Therapeutic Approach for Dysglycemia and Dyslipidemia Control in Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus and Metabolic Syndrome

benefits.p: Sage naturally contains polyphenols, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which help protect cells from oxidative stress. These plant compounds contribute to the herb's nutritional value alongside its vitamins and minerals.

ref.s: European Food Information Council (EUFIC)

benefits.p: Fresh sage is a good source of vitamin K, a nutrient needed for normal blood clotting and the maintenance of healthy bones. Even small amounts can contribute to dietary vitamin K intake.

ref.s: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements

benefits.p: Sage naturally provides manganese, an essential trace mineral that contributes to normal energy metabolism, the formation of connective tissue and the maintenance of healthy bones as part of a varied diet.

ref.s: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements

benefits.p: Sardines are one of the richest natural sources of the marine omega-3 fatty acids EPA and DHA. These healthy fats help maintain normal heart function and are an important part of a balanced diet.

ref.s: NIH Office of Dietary Supplements – Omega-3 Fatty Acids Fact Sheet

benefits.p: When eaten with their soft edible bones, sardines provide highly absorbable calcium along with vitamin D and phosphorus. Together these nutrients help maintain normal bones and teeth.

ref.s: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health – Vitamin D

benefits.h: Rich in vitamin B12 and protein
benefits.p: Sardines provide high-quality protein for maintaining muscle, alongside exceptionally high amounts of vitamin B12, which supports normal energy metabolism, nervous system function and healthy red blood cell formation.

ref.s: Eating more sardines instead of fish oil supplementation: Beyond omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids – PubMed

benefits.p: Pomegranate is naturally rich in polyphenols, including punicalagins and anthocyanins. These plant compounds help protect cells from oxidative stress and contribute to the body's natural antioxidant defences as part of a healthy diet.

ref.s: Potent health effects of pomegranate

benefits.p: Pomegranate provides polyphenols that help support healthy blood vessel function and contribute to cardiovascular health. Regular consumption, particularly as juice, has been shown to support normal blood pressure and vascular function in human studies.

ref.s: Phytotherapy Research – The effects of pomegranate consumption on blood pressure: A systematic review and meta-analysis

benefits.p: The edible arils provide dietary fibre, which supports healthy digestion, contributes to regular bowel function and helps nourish a healthy gut microbiome as part of a balanced diet.

ref.s: USDA Agricultural Research Service – Nutritive Value of Pomegranate Fruit and Juice

benefits.p: Traditional sourdough fermentation can slow starch digestion, helping produce a smaller rise in blood glucose after meals than comparable refined breads. The effect depends on the flour used and the fermentation process.

ref.s: EFSA Journal

benefits.p: The natural fermentation process breaks down some phytic acid, making minerals such as iron, zinc and magnesium more available for absorption. Wholegrain sourdough provides the greatest nutritional advantage.

ref.s: Frontiers in Nutrition

benefits.p: Garlic naturally contains organosulfur compounds such as allicin, which have been associated with supporting normal cardiovascular function. Regular garlic consumption as part of a balanced diet may contribute to maintaining healthy blood pressure and cholesterol levels.

ref.s: National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health (NCCIH)

benefits.p: Garlic is rich in sulfur-containing phytochemicals that help protect cells from oxidative stress. Crushing or chopping garlic activates many of these naturally occurring compounds before cooking.

ref.s: Linus Pauling Institute, Oregon State University

benefits.p: Garlic contains naturally occurring bioactive compounds that help support normal immune responses. Although it is typically eaten in small amounts, it contributes beneficial phytochemicals alongside a varied, balanced diet.

ref.s: Nutrition Research Reviews
